vulnerability.title: Ensure PAM Enforces Password Requirements - Prevent the Use of Dictionary Words
vulnerability.description: The pam_pwquality module's dictcheck check if passwords contains dictionary words. When dictcheck is set to 1 passwords will be checked for dictionary words.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { rpm --quiet -q libpwquality; }; then var_password_pam_dictcheck='' if grep -sq dictcheck /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf ; then sed -i "/dictcheck/d" /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf fi # Strip any search characters in the key arg so that the key can be replaced without # adding any search characters to the config file. stripped_key=$(sed 's/[\^=\$,;+]*//g' <<< "^dictcheck") # shellcheck disable=SC2059 printf -v formatted_output "%s = %s" "$stripped_key" "$var_password_pam_dictcheck" # If the key exists, change it. Otherwise, add it to the config_file. # We search for the key string followed by a word boundary (matched by \>), # so if we search for 'setting', 'setting2' won't match. if LC_AL

triage_decision.reason: The remediation involves editing the PAM password quality configuration to enable dictionary word checking, a standard STIG hardening step that does not affect core services or risk lockout.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Enforced password policy may reject existing passwords that contain dictionary words

llm_verdict.message:
**Remediation applied.**

The dictionary‑word check has been enabled in the PAM password quality configuration:

- `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/10-minlen.conf`  
  `dictcheck = 1` appended

- `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/10-ocredit.conf`  
  `dictcheck = 1` appended

- `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/lcredit.conf`  
  `dictcheck = 1` appended

These changes enable `pam_pwquality.so` to reject passwords containing dictionary words, satisfying the *accounts_password_pam_dictcheck* rule.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will successfully enable dictionary word checking by ensuring `dictcheck = 1` is present in all `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/*.conf` files or by creating a new config file when none exist. While the approach is not the most streamlined, it is functional and addresses the core requirement of the vulnerability. Minor improvements are suggested to increase robustness and idempotency.
concerns: (1) The plan assumes the `/etc/security/pwquality.conf.d/` directory exists; if it does not, `ls` will fail and the subsequent creation step might not be executed. (2) The sed pattern removes the entire line when `dictcheck` is present, which may inadvertently strip other whitespace comments or context that the user may have defined. (3) There is no check that the required pam_pwquality package (`libpwquality`) is installed; modifying the config without the module present is a no‑op but may create confusion. (4) The plan does not verify that `pam_pwquality.so` is actually invoked by the system's PAM configuration, so the change may not take effect if the module is not configured in the appropriate PAM stack.
suggested_improvements:
Add a preliminary check: `mkdir -p /etc/security/pwquality.conf.d` to ensure the directory exists before listing or writing files. (5) Before modifying a file, perform a `grep -q '^
*dictcheck ' <file>` to confirm the presence of the setting and use a more conservative `sed` command that only replaces the setting portion, preserving surrounding context: `sed -i 's/^
*#
*
*dictcheck[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*[^#]*/dictcheck = 1/' <file>`. (6) Verify package presence: `if ! rpm -q libpwquality > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo 'Module not installed’; fi`. (7) Optionally backup the original file before modifying it to avoid accidental loss of configuration.
